message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I'm implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter in filters.py. What file(s) do you need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My feature (with_counts param for groupby filter) touches src/jinja2/filters.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also filters.py, we need to coordinate carefully on non-overlapping regions or sequential edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to edit sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in the same file - can't make this disjoint. Proposal: you go first since you already started. Implement case_sensitive and send DONE. I will wait, then pull your changes and add with_counts on top (new last positional/keyword param), avoiding clobbering your diff. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in filters.py - true overlap, can't fully split by file. Proposal: my case_sensitive change only touches the sort key (adds ignore_case to expr) and normalizes the group key preserving original case in output - it does NOT touch the count/output-tuple construction. Your with_counts sounds additive to the output (adding a count alongside grouper/list). Let's sequence: I implement case_sensitive first and post my exact diff here, then you rebase your with_counts change on top of it so we don't clobber. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed - I'll implement case_sensitive now and send DONE with the diff summary when finished so you can layer with_counts on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. You implement case_sensitive first (touches sort key / make_attrgetter call), post your diff, then I'll add with_counts on top by extending the output tuple construction and function signature. Will wait for your DONE before editing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for sync_do_groupby/do_groupby. Signature changes: added 'case_sensitive: bool = False' as the LAST parameter on both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (after 'default'). Body: expr now built with postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None; after building 'out' list of _GroupTuple, if not case_sensitive we remap each group's key via a case-sensitive output_expr(values[0]) to preserve original casing. Feel free to add your with_counts param after case_sensitive in the signature and extend the _GroupTuple construction - just don't touch the case_sensitive logic. All existing tests pass (122/122 in test_filters.py). Go ahead and rebase your with_counts change on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts param. Since my sandbox copy of filters.py doesn't reflect your case_sensitive edits (looks like we're in isolated environments), I implemented with_counts independently on top of the original file: added 'with_counts: bool = False' as the last param on both sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, and a new _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count) namedtuple used only when with_counts=True (kept _GroupTuple as-is for the default 2-tuple case to avoid breaking existing unpacking). All 122 filter tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt.
